info.title: Next Wiki API
info.description: OpenAPI specification for the Next Wiki REST API.

name.description: Human-readable name for the API key.

scopes.description: Permission scopes granted to the key.

keyPrefix.description: Non-secret prefix of the key, shown for identification.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the key was created.

revokedAt.description: Timestamp when the key was revoked, or null if still active.

lastUsedAt.description: Timestamp when the key was last used, or null if never used.

keySecret.description: Full secret key value. Shown only once, at creation time.

ApiKeyCreated.description: API key creation response, including the one-time secret value.

name.description: Human-readable name for the API key.

scopes.description: Permission scopes granted to the key.

keyPrefix.description: Non-secret prefix of the key, shown for identification.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the key was created.

revokedAt.description: Timestamp when the key was revoked, or null if still active.

lastUsedAt.description: Timestamp when the key was last used, or null if never used.

keyId.description: API key identifier used for the request, or null when not applicable.

keyName.description: API key name used for the request, or null when not applicable.

userId.description: Identifier of the user tied to the request, or null when not applicable.

userEmail.description: Email of the user tied to the request, or null when not applicable.

errorMessage.description: Error message, or null when the request succeeded.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the entry was recorded.

entries.description: Audit entries for the current result window.

total.description: Total number of matching audit entries.

backendId.description: Identifier of a saved backend to check.

type.description: Backend type to check, when checking an ad-hoc configuration.

config.description: Ad-hoc backend configuration to check, when not checking a saved backend.

secret.description: Secret for the ad-hoc configuration, if required.

BackendCheckInput.description: Ephemeral storage backend connection check. Provide either a saved backendId or an ad-hoc type/config pair.

detail.description: Additional detail, typically present on failure.

BackendCheckResult.description: Result of a storage backend connection check.

ChangeEmailInput.description: Change the current user email address.

currentPassword.description: Current password, required to authorize the change.

backendId.description: Identifier of the backend being cleaned up.

totalItems.description: Total number of items to delete.

deletedItems.description: Number of items deleted so far.

errorMessage.description: Error message, or null if none.

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the cleanup job started, or null if not yet started.

finishedAt.description: Timestamp when the cleanup job finished, or null if still running.

backendId.description: Identifier of the backend to delete migrated-away content from.

confirm.description: Must be true to confirm the destructive cleanup.

CleanupStartInput.description: Start a storage backend cleanup job.

name.description: Human-readable name for the API key.

scopes.description: Permission scopes granted to the key. At least one is required; scopes must be unique.

queued.description: Whether a Git export run was queued.

GitExportRunResult.description: Result of manually triggering a Git export run.

remoteUrl.description: Git remote URL (HTTPS or SSH, without embedded credentials).

branch.description: Git branch to push exports to.

publicKey.description: SSH public key, when using SSH authentication.

fingerprint.description: SSH key fingerprint, when using SSH authentication.

autoSyncOnPublish.description: Whether to export automatically when a page is published. Defaults to true.

scheduledSyncEnabled.description: Whether to export on a schedule. Defaults to false.

scheduledSyncIntervalMinutes.description: Interval in minutes between scheduled exports (5-1440). Defaults to 60.

secret.description: Git HTTPS access token. Write-only; never echoed back.

GitExportUpsert.description: Create or update the Git export backend configuration.

GitSshKeyResult.description: Generated Git export SSH key pair (public half).

mustResetPassword.description: Whether the user must set a new password before continuing.

displayName.description: Current user display name, or null when not set.

MigrationList.description: List of content storage migration jobs.

targetBackendId.description: Identifier of the backend to migrate content into.

confirmOverwrite.description: Must be true to proceed when the target backend already has content.

abortRequested.description: Whether an abort has been requested for this migration.

totalItems.description: Total number of items to migrate.

copiedItems.description: Number of items copied so far.

verifiedItems.description: Number of items verified so far.

errorMessage.description: Error message, or null if none.

startedAt.description: Timestamp when the migration started, or null if not yet started.

finishedAt.description: Timestamp when the migration finished, or null if still running.

theme.description: Preferred theme, or null when using the default.

locale.description: Preferred locale, or null when using the default.

contentType.description: MIME type of the stored asset.

sizeBytes.description: Size of the asset in bytes.

url.description: URL to fetch the asset content.

markdown.description: Ready-to-paste Markdown image snippet referencing this asset.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the asset was created (ISO 8601).

contentType.description: MIME type of the uploaded asset.

sizeBytes.description: Size of the uploaded asset in bytes.

url.description: URL to fetch the uploaded asset content.

markdown.description: Ready-to-paste Markdown image snippet referencing the uploaded asset.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the asset was created (ISO 8601).

id.description: Author user identifier, or null when the author is not disclosed.

displayName.description: Author display name, or null when not available.

PublicAuthor.description: Public author identity visible to the caller.

pageId.description: Identifier of the page containing the link.

path.description: Canonical path of the page containing the link.

title.description: Title of the page containing the link.

linkText.description: Link text used in the Markdown link.

PublicBacklink.description: A single backlink to a target page.

items.description: Pages visible to the caller that link to the target page.

path.description: Canonical path of the created page.

revisionId.description: Identifier of the initial draft revision.

PublicBatchCreateResult.description: Result of a batch page creation request.

title.description: Human-readable title for the draft revision.

contentSource.description: Markdown source of the draft revision.

baseRevisionId.description: Revision the draft is based on, for conflict detection. Omit to base on the latest revision.

baseContentHash.description: Expected content hash of the base revision, used for optimistic concurrency control.

PublicDraftCreateInput.description: Create a draft revision for an existing public page.

locale.description: Locale of the page content (e.g. "en"). Defaults to the workspace default.

contentSource.description: Markdown source of the initial page revision. Optional; defaults to an empty draft.

include.description: Comma-separated relations to include in the returned page resource: latestRevision, publishedRevision.

PublicPageIncludeQuery.description: Optional ?include= query parameter for endpoints returning a single PublicPageResource.

PublicPageIncludeValue.description: Optional page relation that can be requested via ?include=.

status.description: Filter pages by lifecycle state. Defaults to published.

q.description: Optional free-text query to filter pages by path or title.

path.description: Exact canonical path to look up a single page. When provided, returns at most one matching page and ignores other filters.

pathPrefix.description: Directory prefix to list all pages under a subtree (e.g. "docs" matches "docs/a", "docs/b", "docs"). Cannot be combined with path.

limit.description: Maximum number of pages to return per request (1-100). Defaults to 20.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous response.

order.description: Sort order: alphabetical by path, or most recently updated first. Defaults to path.

include.description: Comma-separated relations to include: latestRevision, publishedRevision. Omitted by default; fetch a specific revision via GET /pages/{id}/revisions/{version} instead.

items.description: Page resources for the current result window.

nextCursor.description: Cursor for the next page of results, or null when there are no more.

PublicPagePath.description: Canonical wiki page path. Use lowercase slash-separated path segments.

baseRevisionId.description: Expected current revision id, used for optimistic concurrency control.

PublicPagePropertiesInput.description: Update page properties. Provide at least one of path or title.

spaceSlug.description: Slug of the wiki space the page belongs to.

contentSource.description: Markdown source of the current revision. Omitted from list/search results; present on single-page reads and writes.

status.description: Page lifecycle state: an unpublished draft, a published page, or a soft-deleted page.

latestRevision.description: Most recent revision of any status, or null if none exists. Omitted unless requested via ?include=latestRevision.

publishedRevision.description: Most recent published revision, or null if never published. Omitted unless requested via ?include=publishedRevision.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the page was created (ISO 8601).

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the page was last updated (ISO 8601).

self.description: Canonical API URL of this page resource. Also the target for PATCH updates.

byPath.description: API URL to look up this page by its canonical path (GET /v1/pages?path=...).

revisions.description: API URL listing this page revisions.

drafts.description: API URL for creating draft revisions of this page.

links.description: Related API resource URLs for this page.

scope.description: Restrict matching to a field, or search across all fields. Defaults to all.

status.description: Filter results by page lifecycle state. Defaults to published.

pathPrefix.description: Directory prefix to restrict matching to pages under a subtree (e.g. "docs" matches "docs/a", "docs/b").

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return per request (1-100). Defaults to 20.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous response.

include.description: Comma-separated relations to include on each result page: latestRevision, publishedRevision.

excerptLength.description: Approximate number of characters of context to return around the matched keyword in excerpt (20-500). Defaults to 100.

createdStart.description: Only include pages created at or after this ISO 8601 timestamp.

createdEnd.description: Only include pages created at or before this ISO 8601 timestamp.

updatedStart.description: Only include pages last updated at or after this ISO 8601 timestamp.

updatedEnd.description: Only include pages last updated at or before this ISO 8601 timestamp.

items.description: Search results for the current result window.

nextCursor.description: Cursor for the next page of results, or null when there are no more.

path.description: Full canonical path of this node (may be empty for the root).

segment.description: Last slash-separated segment of the path.

title.description: Page title when a page exists at this path, otherwise null.

pageId.description: Page id when a page exists at this path, otherwise null.

status.description: Page status when a page exists, otherwise null.

children.description: Child nodes ordered by path segment.

PublicPageTreeNode.description: A single node in the public wiki page directory tree.

status.description: Filter pages by lifecycle state. Defaults to published.

pathPrefix.description: Directory prefix to scope the tree to a subtree (e.g. "docs" returns only the docs/ branch).

root.description: Root node of the directory tree. When pathPrefix is set, this is the prefix node.

pageCount.description: Total number of visible pages represented in the tree.

PublicPageTreeResponse.description: Hierarchical directory structure of public wiki pages.

expectedRevisionId.description: Revision id the caller expects to publish, used for optimistic concurrency control.

against.description: Earlier version number to diff against.

diff.description: Unified diff between the two revision sources.

PublicRevisionDiffResponse.description: Structured diff between two page revisions.

status.description: Filter revisions by lifecycle state. Defaults to all.

limit.description: Maximum number of revisions to return per request (1-100). Defaults to 20.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous response.

items.description: Revision resources for the current result window.

nextCursor.description: Cursor for the next page of results, or null when there are no more.

pageId.description: Identifier of the page this revision belongs to.

version.description: Monotonically increasing revision version, starting at 1.

status.description: Revision lifecycle state: an unpublished draft or a published revision.

contentType.description: Content media type of the revision body. Always text/markdown.

contentHash.description: Content hash of the Markdown source, used for optimistic concurrency control.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the revision was created (ISO 8601).

publishedAt.description: Timestamp when the revision was published (ISO 8601), or null if still a draft.

canPublish.description: Whether the current caller is allowed to publish this revision.

contentSource.description: Markdown source of the revision. Present only on GET /pages/{id}/revisions/{version}; omitted from the revision list.

pageId.description: Identifier of the page this revision belongs to.

version.description: Monotonically increasing revision version, starting at 1.

status.description: Revision lifecycle state: an unpublished draft or a published revision.

contentType.description: Content media type of the revision body. Always text/markdown.

contentHash.description: Content hash of the Markdown source, used for optimistic concurrency control.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the revision was created (ISO 8601).

publishedAt.description: Timestamp when the revision was published (ISO 8601), or null if still a draft.

canPublish.description: Whether the current caller is allowed to publish this revision.

matchType.description: Which field produced the match for this result.

excerpt.description: Snippet of contentSource centered on the matched keyword (~excerptLength characters), or null for path/title matches or when not applicable. The result page never includes the full contentSource.

score.description: Relevance score in (0, 1]. Path matches score highest, then title, then content (weighted by how many times the term appears). Results are sorted by score descending within each returned page; null is not currently produced.

results.description: Candidate similar pages sorted by score descending.

threshold.description: Threshold applied to the result set.

include.description: Optional additional report; "orphans" lists pages with zero inbound links.

publishedPages.description: Published pages visible to the caller.

draftPages.description: Draft pages visible to the caller.

deletedPages.description: Soft-deleted pages visible to the caller.

createdInLast7Days.description: Pages created in the last 7 days.

updatedInLast7Days.description: Pages updated in the last 7 days.

orphans.description: Pages with no inbound links, when include=orphans is requested.

email.description: Email address for the new account.

state.description: Replication lifecycle state of this backend.

totalItems.description: Total number of items to replicate.

completedItems.description: Number of items replicated so far.

failedItems.description: Number of items that failed to replicate.

lastError.description: Most recent error message, or null if none.

tempPassword.description: Temporary password issued to the user (8-128 characters).

SetMyPasswordInput.description: Set a new password for the current user (e.g. after a forced reset).

role.description: Role to assign to the target user.

status.description: Status to assign to the target user.

email.description: Administrator email address for the initial account.

SetupInput.description: First-run setup input, creating the initial administrator account.

retainData.description: Whether to keep existing content on the backend instead of deleting it.

syncExisting.description: Whether to backfill existing content onto the backend.

type.description: Use the database as the storage backend.

config.description: Database backend has no additional configuration.

type.description: Use the local filesystem as the storage backend.

basePath.description: Base directory path for stored content.

type.description: Use S3-compatible object storage as the storage backend.

endpoint.description: Custom S3-compatible endpoint URL, if not using AWS.

prefix.description: Optional key prefix for stored objects.

config.description: S3 backend configuration. The secret access key is submitted separately via `secret`.

secret.description: S3 secret access key. Write-only; never echoed back.

StorageBackendUpsert.description: Storage backend configuration write. The shape of `config` depends on `type`.

purpose.description: Role this backend plays: authoritative content store, or Git export target.

isActive.description: Whether this backend is the currently active one for its purpose.

replicaState.description: Replication lifecycle state of this backend.

isReadPreferred.description: Whether reads are preferentially served from this backend.

syncStartedAt.description: Timestamp when the current sync started, or null if none is running.

syncCompletedAt.description: Timestamp when the last sync completed, or null if never completed.

lastSyncAt.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful sync, or null if never synced.

lastError.description: Most recent error message, or null if none.

hasSecret.description: Whether a secret (e.g. S3 key or Git token) is stored for this backend.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the backend was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the backend was last updated.

StorageBackendView.description: Storage backend view, as returned to the admin UI. Never includes secrets.

active.description: Backend currently active for reads and writes.

authoritative.description: Backend that is the authoritative content store.

preferredReadBackend.description: Backend preferentially used for reads, or null to use the authoritative backend.

gitExport.description: Git export backend, or null if not configured.

migration.description: In-progress or most recent migration, or null if none.

ssl.description: Whether SSL is enabled for the database connection.

containerPath.description: Local storage path inside the container.

hostPath.description: Local storage path on the host, or null when unknown.

deployment.description: Deployment information for the database and local storage backends.

backendId.description: Backend to prefer for reads, or null to use the authoritative backend.

theme.description: Preferred theme, or null to reset to the default.

locale.description: Preferred locale, or null to reset to the default.

displayName.description: New display name, or null to clear it.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of all API audit entries. Admin only.

get.summary: List AI action audit @tag AI @auth bearer

post.summary: Generate a private Wiki illustration preview @tag AI @auth bearer
Optimize selected Wiki Markdown @tag AI @auth bearer
Ask a grounded Wiki question @tag AI @auth bearer
Start semantic Wiki search @tag AI @auth bearer

get.summary: List my AI chat sessions @tag AI @auth bearer
Get AI action @tag AI @auth bearer @response AiActionView
Get my effective AI entitlement @tag AI @auth bearer
Preview a private generated image @tag AI @auth bearer

delete.summary: Discard a private generated image @tag AI @auth bearer

get.summary: Get one of my AI chat sessions @tag AI @auth bearer

delete.summary: Permanently delete one of my AI chat sessions @tag AI @auth bearer @response 204

get.summary: Stream AI action events @tag AI @auth bearer

post.summary: Promote a generated image into a Wiki asset @tag AI @auth bearer

get.summary: List AI indexes @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

post.summary: Start AI index rebuild @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

get.summary: List AI models @tag AI Admin @auth bearer
List AI providers @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

post.summary: Create AI provider @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

get.summary: Get AI settings @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

patch.summary: Update AI settings @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

get.summary: AI usage statistics @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

put.summary: Assign AI purpose model @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

get.summary: Get user AI entitlement @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

put.summary: Update user AI entitlement @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

get.summary: Get AI index @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

delete.summary: Delete AI index @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

patch.summary: Update AI model @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

delete.summary: Delete AI model @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

get.summary: Get AI provider @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

patch.summary: Update AI provider @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

delete.description: Cascades through the provider's models, purpose assignments, index generations, and completed run records. Active runs prevent deletion.

post.summary: Test an AI provider connection synchronously @tag AI Admin @auth bearer
Cancel building AI index @tag AI Admin @auth bearer @response 204
Retry AI index pages @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

get.summary: List AI index page states @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

post.summary: Create manual AI model @tag AI Admin @auth bearer
Test AI provider @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

put.summary: Override AI model capability @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

delete.summary: Remove AI model capability override @tag AI Admin @auth bearer

get.description: Lists all system themes (built-ins + custom) and the active selection. Requires manage_appearance.

post.description: Copies an existing theme (typically a built-in) into a new editable custom row. Requires manage_appearance.

get.description: Returns the public site identity and footer settings.

put.description: Updates the site name and footer fields. Requires the manage_appearance capability.

get.description: Returns the full system theme (id, name, css, isBuiltin). Requires manage_appearance.

put.description: Updates a custom theme's name and/or CSS. Built-ins are read-only. Requires manage_appearance.

delete.description: Deletes a custom theme. Built-ins cannot be deleted. If the deleted theme was active, the active pointer is cleared. Requires manage_appearance.

put.description: Sets the active system theme. Pass null to clear the active selection. Requires manage_appearance.

get.description: Returns the per-user reading-theme tokens (or defaults if the user has not customized). Authenticated.

put.description: Replaces the per-user reading-theme tokens. Authenticated.

delete.summary: Reset the caller's reading-theme tokens to defaults
delete.description: Deletes the per-user row; subsequent reads return the static defaults. Authenticated.

get.description: Serves the configured site icon, or redirects to the shipped default.

put.description: Uploads a custom site icon (SVG/PNG/ICO). Requires the manage_appearance capability.

delete.description: Reverts the site icon to the shipped default. Requires the manage_appearance capability.

get.description: Streams the bytes of an image asset. Requires read access to a page that references it; unreadable or missing assets return 404 with no existence leak.

post.description: Uploads an image asset and returns a Markdown-ready public reference.

get.description: Returns asset metadata if the caller may read the asset.

schema.description: Stable public asset identifier.

get.description: Streams asset bytes if the caller may read the asset.

schema.description: Stable public asset identifier.

post.description: Authenticates with email and password and establishes a session.
Destroys the current session.

get.description: Returns the currently authenticated session user, or null when anonymous.

post.description: Creates a new user account and establishes a session.
Allows a signed-in user to set a new password after an admin reset.
Creates the first admin user when the wiki has no users yet.

get.description: Returns the generated OpenAPI 3.1 JSON document describing all public REST endpoints.
Lists pages visible to the caller through the stable Public Wiki Content API. List

get.description: Returns the active backend, all configured primary backends, the Git export target, and any in-progress migration. Admin only; secrets are never included.

put.description: Creates or updates a primary backend's non-secret configuration and optional write-only secret. Does not activate it. Admin only.

post.description: Validates configuration and runs a health check against a saved backend or an ad-hoc config, without changing any backend state. Admin only.
Starts a confirmed cleanup of an inactive backend's retained data. Admin only.

get.description: Returns the current Git sync backend view (masked secrets) for status polling. Admin only.

put.description: Configures and enables or disables the one-way published-content Git export. Secrets are write-only. Enabling queues a full snapshot export.

get.description: Returns recent migrations. Admin only.

post.description: Starts a safe copy-verify-cutover migration to the target backend and returns immediately with the migration id. Admin only.

put.description: Selects an enabled replica for preferred reads, or Database when backendId is null. Admin only.

get.description: Returns the progress of a retained-backend cleanup job. Admin only.

post.description: Cancels pending git-export jobs and clears a sync stuck in backfilling.
Queues an asynchronous full reconciliation of published Markdown and referenced assets.
Generates or rotates the server Ed25519 key. The encrypted private key remains server-side; only the public key and fingerprint are returned.

get.description: Returns the progress of a migration for polling. Admin only.

delete.description: Requests a cooperative abort; the worker stops at its next checkpoint and never cuts over after the request. Admin only.

post.description: Removes a replica from read and write routing. Optionally schedules deletion of retained replica data. Admin only.
Health-checks a configured replica and starts an idempotent Database backfill. Admin only.

get.description: Returns Database-to-replica backfill progress and errors. Admin only.

get.description: Returns the hierarchical directory structure of pages visible to the caller.

parameters.name: status

schema.description: Filter pages by lifecycle state. Defaults to published.

parameters.description: Filter pages by lifecycle state. Defaults to published.

parameters.name: pathPrefix

schema.description: Directory prefix to scope the tree to a subtree (e.g. "docs" returns only the docs/ branch).

parameters.description: Directory prefix to scope the tree to a subtree (e.g. "docs" returns only the docs/ branch).

get.description: Returns the current user's API keys without secrets. Session-only; not callable with a Bearer key.

post.description: Creates a new API key with the requested scopes. Session-only; not callable with a Bearer key. The full secret is returned only once.

get.description: Returns a paginated list of API audit entries for the signed-in user.

delete.description: Revokes one of the user's API keys. Session-only; not callable with a Bearer key.

patch.description: Changes the signed-in user's email address.

post.description: Changes the signed-in user's password after verifying the current password.

patch.description: Updates the signed-in user's theme and/or locale preference.
Updates the signed-in user's display name.

get.description: Decrypts and returns the full secret for one of the user's API keys. Session-only; not callable with a Bearer key.

post.description: Resets the password of the specified user and forces a reset on next login. Admin only.
Changes the role of the specified user. Admin only.
Enables or disables the specified user. Admin only.
